title: Rogue Doctrine
content:
{{Top|leg}}
'''Rogue Doctrine''' was the plan of attack designed by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] tactician [[Beryl Chiffonage]], with help from [[Risiev Credal]], for use by the [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeders]] stationed at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].

==The plan==
[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Skywalker]] and [[Beryl Chiffonage|Chiffonage]] knew they would be facing [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] in the coming battle after the location [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] was discovered by the Empire. To that end, they developed three tactics to face the invading force of [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] walkers while attempting to buy time for the base's [[Transport/Legends|transports]] to escape.

===Attack Pattern Delta===
[[File:Attack_Pattern_Delta_TCG.png|thumb|left|200px|Attack Pattern Delta in action]]
{{Main|Attack Pattern Delta/Legends}}

This tactic involved a group of snowspeeders attacking an AT-AT in a single-file formation in order to give the enemy gunners a single target. After reaching optimal firing range, the lead speeder would fire before breaking off. With the AT-AT only able to track one target at a time, the remaining speeders would have a clear shot at the walker before parting in different directions.

===Bantha Decoy===
{{Main|Bantha Decoy}}

The second tactic was a variant of a trick used by Skywalker to target [[Bantha/Legends|banthas]] in his [[T-16 skyhopper/Legends|T-16 skyhopper]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Two speeders would approach a walker from behind while flanking it. The lead speeder would cut across the walker's line of sight to draw its fire, while simultaneously veering across the second speeder's flight vector. This would expose the vulnerable neck of the AT-AT to the second speeder, creating a perfect opportunity to attack.
[[File:HarpoonTowCable-SWI33.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The harpoon and two cable method in action]]

===Harpoons and tow cables===
{{Main|Power harpoon launcher}}
While an AT-AT was a vicious monster of destruction, it was still a mere machine. Using the harpoons and tow cables of the T-47 to entangle the walker's legs, the giant could effectively be tripped, bringing its own weight crashing down upon itself. This third plan of attack worked most effectively during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. Several AT-ATs were brought down using this method.

==Sources==
